message:
feat(doctor): add --json output and refactor diagnostic rendering

Restructure the wa doctor command for dual text/JSON output:

- Add --json flag to Doctor command for machine-readable diagnostics,
  enabling automation and CI integration
- Refactor diagnostic flow: collect all checks (environment, runtime
  health, crash history, circuit breakers) into vectors before output,
  instead of printing inline during evaluation. This decouples check
  evaluation from rendering.
- Add DiagnosticCheck::to_json_value() method for structured JSON
  serialization of check results (name, status, detail, recommendation)
- Add DiagnosticStatus::as_str() for clean string conversion
- JSON output includes: ok (bool), status (ok|warning|error), version,
  checks array, and optional circuits array with state/failure counts
- Merge Ok and Info health diagnostic variants into a single arm
  (both produce ok_with_detail, reducing match duplication)
- Compute has_errors/has_warnings from the complete check collection
  after all checks are gathered, rather than mutating flags inline
- Fix verbose field type from bool to u8 in test setup to match
  updated RemoteSetupOptions definition

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
